name: Sparse Set

x.invariant: a dense array of present keys plus a sparse array mapping key→index into the dense array; the two stay mutually consistent.
x.memoryOverhead: A sparse array sized to the key domain (can be large) plus a dense array sized to the live count.
x.locality: Excellent: iteration walks the contiguous dense array; ideal for SIMD and cache prefetch.
x.mutationModel: Mutable in place; removal swaps the last element into the hole (does not preserve order).
x.concurrency: Not thread-safe by default; per-archetype use in ECS is typically single-writer.

def: An integer-keyed set/map giving O(1) insert, remove and membership with contiguous, cache-friendly iteration — the backbone of many ECS component stores.
problem: Track a dynamic subset of small integer ids (entities) with fast add/remove and fast dense iteration.
mechanism: Keeps a dense array of live keys and a sparse lookup array (key→dense index). Removal swaps the last dense element into the gap and fixes its sparse entry, all in O(1).
limits: Sparse array sized to the key domain (memory)
Iteration order is unstable after removals
failures: Huge key domain wastes the sparse array
Forgetting to fix the swapped element corrupts the set
whenNot: Keys are arbitrary / large (use a hash set)
myths: "Same as a bitset" — it also gives O(1) dense iteration of present keys
